name: Claude Opus 4.8
description: Anthropic's top-tier flagship; strongest reasoning and long-horizon agentic coding, highest cost.

name: Claude Opus 4.7
description: Previous-generation Anthropic flagship; strong reasoning and long-horizon agentic coding.

name: Claude Sonnet 4.6
description: Anthropic's mid-tier flagship; balanced coding performance and cost, widely used in agentic coding tools.

name: Claude Haiku 4.5
description: Anthropic's fast, low-cost model; good for high-throughput coding tasks and simple agent loops.

name: Claude Opus 4.5
description: Previous-generation Anthropic flagship; still strong on complex reasoning tasks.

name: Claude Sonnet 4.5
description: Previous-generation Anthropic mid-tier; baseline for many agentic coding benchmarks.

name: Claude 3.7 Sonnet
description: Hybrid reasoning model from Anthropic; introduced extended thinking. Older but still in production use.

name: GPT-5
description: OpenAI's flagship general-purpose model; strong coding and tool-use capabilities.

name: GPT-5 mini
description: Smaller, cheaper, faster variant of GPT-5; popular default for cost-sensitive agentic use.

name: GPT-5 nano
description: Smallest GPT-5 variant; cheapest, fastest, lower capability ceiling.

name: o3
description: OpenAI's reasoning model; strong on math, code, and step-by-step problem solving.

name: o4-mini
description: Smaller, cheaper OpenAI reasoning model; competitive on coding benchmarks at lower cost than o3.

name: GPT-4.1
description: Coding-tuned GPT-4 generation from OpenAI; still common in pipelines and tools.

name: Gemini 2.5 Pro
description: Google's flagship reasoning model; very long context, strong on code and multimodal tasks.

name: Gemini 2.5 Flash
description: Cheaper, faster Gemini variant; balances cost and capability for coding agents.

name: Gemini 2.5 Flash-Lite
description: Smallest Gemini 2.5; cheapest token cost, suitable for high-volume light tasks.

name: Gemini 3 Pro
description: Google's next-generation flagship; improved reasoning and agentic coding over 2.5.

name: Grok 4
description: xAI's flagship model; strong on reasoning and coding, available via x.ai and OpenRouter.

name: Grok 4 Fast
description: Cheaper, faster Grok 4 variant; popular for cost-sensitive coding agent runs.

name: DeepSeek V3.2
description: DeepSeek's hosted flagship; strong coding model at competitive pricing.

name: DeepSeek R1
description: DeepSeek's reasoning model; widely used via the DeepSeek API and inference platforms.

name: Mistral Large 2
description: Mistral's hosted flagship; general-purpose model with respectable coding ability.

name: Codestral 2
description: Mistral's coding-specialized hosted model; designed for IDE and agentic coding workflows.

name: Command A
description: Cohere's flagship enterprise model; tool-use and reasoning oriented.

name: Llama 3.3 70B Instruct
description: Meta's open-weight 70B instruct model; widely available across self-hosted and platform inference.

name: Llama 3.1 405B Instruct
description: Meta's largest open-weight Llama 3.1 release; strong general capability, heavy infra needs.

name: Llama 3.1 8B Instruct
description: Small open-weight Llama 3.1; common baseline for local runs and resource-constrained setups.

name: Qwen2.5 Coder 32B Instruct
description: Alibaba Qwen's coding-tuned open-weight model; popular for self-hosted coding agents.

name: Qwen2.5 Coder 7B Instruct
description: Small Qwen2.5 Coder variant; runs on modest hardware while remaining competitive for coding tasks.

name: Qwen3 Coder 30B
description: Newer Qwen3-generation coding-focused open-weight model.

name: Qwen3 235B A22B
description: Large Qwen3 MoE model; 22B active params, used as a flagship open-weight general model.

name: DeepSeek V3 (open-weight)
description: DeepSeek's open-weight V3 release; strong general capability, common in self-hosted setups.

name: DeepSeek R1 Distill Qwen 32B
description: Distilled R1 reasoning model on a Qwen 32B base; popular open-weight reasoning model.

name: DeepSeek R1 Distill Llama 70B
description: Distilled R1 on a Llama 70B base; alternative open-weight reasoning model.

name: Codestral 22B
description: Mistral's open-weight coding model; popular for self-hosted code completion and small agents.

name: Mistral Large Instruct (2411)
description: Mistral's open-weight large instruct release; competitive open alternative to hosted flagships.

name: Gemma 3 27B IT
description: Google's open-weight Gemma 3 instruction-tuned model; runs well on a single high-end GPU.

name: Phi-4
description: Microsoft's small open-weight model; punches above its parameter count on reasoning benchmarks.
